name: KBR National Park
description: Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park is a 390-acre protected forest reserve located in the heart of Hyderabad, surrounded by Jubilee Hills and Banjara Hills. The park is a remarkable urban biodiversity hotspot with over 600 species of plants, 140 bird species, monitor lizards, porcupines, jackals, and Indian foxes. A 2.5-kilometre walking trail winds through the forest with benches and bird-watching platforms. The park is one of the few places in the world where a national park exists entirely within a metropolitan city boundary. Morning walks are extremely popular with birders and fitness enthusiasts.
best_time: Early morning 6am-9am for birding and wildlife spotting

name: Mrugavani National Park
description: Mrugavani National Park is a 3.5-square-kilometre protected area located 25 kilometres south of Hyderabad near Chilkur. The park is a natural scrub forest with dry deciduous vegetation, home to blackbuck, chital deer, blue bull, porcupines, and over 100 bird species. The park has well-marked nature trails, an interpretation centre, a children's park, and a small zoo with rescued animals. Unlike the more urban KBR park, Mrugavani has a more wild feel with dense forest sections. It is an excellent half-day nature excursion from the city for families and nature lovers.

name: Osman Sagar Lake and Gandipet
description: Osman Sagar, also known as Gandipet Lake, is a large reservoir built in 1920 on the Musi River, located 23 kilometres from Hyderabad city centre. The lake spreads over 46 square kilometres and was the primary drinking water source for Hyderabad for decades. The lakeside area is a peaceful natural retreat with open grasslands, rocky outcrops, and migratory birds during winter months between November and February. The viewpoint from the dam offers panoramic views of the lake and the Deccan landscape. Boating facilities are available. Best paired with a visit to the adjacent Himayat Sagar lake.
best_time: Morning 7am-10am or winter months for migratory birds

name: Ananthagiri Hills
description: Ananthagiri Hills is a picturesque hill station located 90 kilometres from Hyderabad in the Vikarabad district of Telangana, rising to an altitude of 677 metres. The area is covered in dense teak and bamboo forests, coffee plantations, and waterfalls including Ananthagiri Falls which flows strongest after the monsoon season. The hills are home to the historic Anantha Padmanabha Swamy temple. The surrounding forest is a reserved forest area with trekking trails and wildlife including leopards, deer, and numerous bird species. A popular weekend getaway from Hyderabad for nature lovers.
best_time: Post-monsoon October-November for waterfalls and greenery

name: Ameenpur Lake Bird Sanctuary
description: Ameenpur Lake is a 136-acre lake located 20 kilometres from Hyderabad city centre near Sangareddy highway, declared as a biodiversity heritage site. The lake is a significant birding destination attracting over 100 species of resident and migratory birds including flamingos, pelicans, painted storks, black-necked storks, and various duck species. Between November and February, thousands of migratory birds from Central Asia and Siberia use the lake as a wintering ground. The lake is an excellent and accessible birding site and is largely undiscovered by mainstream tourism, making it peaceful and uncrowded.
best_time: Early morning 6am-9am between November and February for migratory birds

name: Durgam Cheruvu Cable Bridge
description: Durgam Cheruvu, meaning Secret Lake, is a natural freshwater lake located in the heart of Jubilee Hills, surrounded by rocky granite outcrops and urban forest. The 2019-built cable-stayed pedestrian bridge spans 480 metres across the lake and is one of the most impressive pieces of urban infrastructure in Hyderabad. The bridge and lakeside promenade are popular for evening walks, cycling, and photography. The rocky hillsides around the lake have several ancient cave inscriptions and are a favourite with local trekkers. The area is beautifully lit at night and offers dramatic views of the surrounding Hitech City skyline.
best_time: Evening 5pm-8pm for sunset and bridge lighting

name: Pocharam Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Pocharam Wildlife Sanctuary is a 130-square-kilometre protected forest area located 110 kilometres north of Hyderabad near Medak district. The sanctuary encompasses Pocharam Lake, a large reservoir surrounded by dry deciduous forest. Wildlife includes sambar deer, chital, nilgai, wild boar, sloth bears, and over 200 bird species. The lake attracts large flocks of migratory waterfowl during winter. The Pocharam Lake viewpoint from the dam is spectacular during winter mornings with mist over the water. It requires a full day trip from Hyderabad and is ideal for dedicated wildlife and bird enthusiasts.
best_time: Full day trip, best between November and February
